description: In June 2013, Edward Snowden leaked classified documents exposing the NSA's PRISM program, which involved the bulk collection of digital communications from major US tech firms. The revelations sparked a global debate on the balance between national security surveillance and individual digital privacy rights.

key_events.description: The Guardian and Washington Post publish the first leaks regarding PRISM.
Tech giants including Google, Apple, and Facebook issue public letters demanding greater transparency on data requests.
President Obama delivers a major speech at the Department of Justice defending the necessity of the surveillance programs.
The USA Freedom Act is introduced to Congress to curtail bulk data collection practices.

calibration_notes: This event serves as a foundational benchmark for tracking how public perception shifts when high-level technical intelligence activities are exposed, highlighting the divide between security-focused and privacy-focused demographics.
